title: The Fourteenth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## September 3, 2023 | Preacher: Jack Karn

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

September 3, 2023 was the Fourteenth Sunday after Pentecost, placing the church in Ordinary Time (the long season between Pentecost and Advent). The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this Sunday were Exodus 12:1-14 (the Passover institution), Psalm 113, Romans 13:8-14, and Matthew 18:15-20 (Jesus teaching on church discipline and reconciliation). This cluster of texts emphasizes covenant remembrance, communal accountability, and the practice of binding and loosing in the church community.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the months following General Convention 2024 (held in Louisville, Kentucky in June 2023), which had addressed continuing tensions over human sexuality and biblical authority, with Traditional Anglican Communion (TAC) discussions ongoing. In Colorado, Bishop Kimberly Lucas of the Diocese of Colorado was in her fourth year of episcopacy (consecrated 2019), and the diocese was navigating post-pandemic rebuilding. No major Episcopal news specifically from early September 2023 is documented, though the national church remained divided over various theological and polity issues.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing intense late summer heat and early wildfire season concerns as Labor Day approached; Colorado had faced significant fire activity throughout August 2023. The Denver metro area was focused on the imminent start of the academic year at CU Boulder, DU, and metro K-12 schools. Capitol Hill and downtown Denver were in their typical early-September rhythm of post-summer tourism and back-to-school preparation, with no major civic crises immediately preceding this Sunday.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The UAW (United Auto Workers) was preparing for a strike against the Big Three automakers, set to begin September 15, 2023—just two weeks after this sermon. The Biden administration was managing ongoing inflation and interest rate concerns amid Federal Reserve policy decisions. The war in Ukraine remained a steady backdrop of national news, with President Zelenskyy recently visiting Washington (August 2023) to seek additional military aid.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The war in Ukraine continued with Russia holding significant territorial gains in eastern Ukraine despite Ukrainian counteroffensive efforts. Turkey and Syria were still in early stages of humanitarian recovery from the devastating February 2023 earthquakes. Morocco had been hit by a major earthquake just days before this sermon (September 8-9 were the quake dates, so this is pre-event, but seismic activity in the region was a concern).

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The nation was in a state of political fragmentation heading toward the 2024 election cycle, with deep polarization evident across media and public discourse. The end of summer typically brought a cultural reset—fall television, NFL season beginning, and back-to-school commerce in full swing. Denver audiences would have been aware of the Broncos' upcoming NFL season and the lingering cultural debates over DEI initiatives in corporate and educational institutions that characterized mid-2023.
